briefdescription.para: Internal buffer for accumulating textual data.
Translates a ROOT type string to an internal data enumeration.

detaileddescription.para: Translates a ROOT type string to a data_enum value.

root_str
Pointer to the string containing the ROOT type.
The corresponding data_enum value.
This method parses a ROOT type string (e.g., "vector<float>") and returns the corresponding internal data_enum value for type-safe operations.
This function analyzes a ROOT type string and determines the corresponding data_enum value based on the type (float, double, int, etc.) and dimensionality (scalar, vector, vector of vectors).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the ROOT type string.

briefdescription.para: Represents the object's state as a string.

detaileddescription.para: Converts the current data type to a string representation.

A string representation of the object.
This method returns a human-readable string representation of the object's current state, useful for debugging or logging.

simplesect.para: String representation of the data type.

briefdescription.para: Scans and returns the current buffer content.

detaileddescription.para: Scans the buffer and returns a string representation of all set types.

A string containing the current buffer content.
This method returns the current content of the internal buffer, which is used for accumulating textual data during operations.

simplesect.para: String representation of the buffer contents.

detaileddescription.para: Flushes the buffer based on the current type.
This method resets the internal buffer to an empty state, typically called after processing the buffer content.

memberdef.argsstring: (g *&tx, torch::Tensor *data, std::vector< signed long > *s, p prim)
memberdef.name: add_data
